ai_only_rows.comments: Board meeting reviewing the Sponsor A and Hudson's Bay expressions of interest and directing continued exploration of potential transactions. This is the board-level resolution that formally opens the sale-exploration process.
ai_only_rows.source_quote: On April 4, 2013, the board held a regularly scheduled telephonic meeting. At this meeting, Mr. Sadove reviewed with the board the expressions of interest in a potential acquisition of Saks from Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A. The board discussed these expressions of interest, as well as a potential strategic acquisition of Company B. The board directed Mr. Sadove and Saks management to continue exploring these potential transactions.

ai_only_rows.comments: Joint announcement press release by Saks and Hudson's Bay.
ai_only_rows.source_quote: On July 29, 2013, Saks and Hudson’s Bay issued a joint press release announcing entry into the transaction.

ai_rows.comments: Goldman Sachs was Saks' longstanding financial advisor; the earliest narrated advisory engagement in the Background is the December 2012 strategic review.
ai_rows.source_quote: For the past several years, Goldman Sachs, one of Saks’ longstanding financial advisors, has participated in these reviews. One such review took place in December 2012.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate a specific retention date; inferred from first-narrated advisory action (December 2012 strategic review); per §B1 'December 2012' maps to 2012-12-15.
phrase: 'December 2012'

ai_rows.comments: Morgan Stanley described as 'a long-time advisor to Saks' first narrated in the Background on July 26, 2013; no earlier retention date stated.
ai_rows.source_quote: Also on July 26, 2013, the board held a special telephonic meeting attended by representatives of Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley & Co. LLC (a long-time advisor to Saks, referred to as “Morgan Stanley”) and Wachtell Lipton.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate Morgan Stanley's retention date; anchored to earliest Background mention (July 26, 2013).

alex_rows.comments: For the past several years, Goldman Sachs, one of Saks’ longstanding financial advisors, has participated in these reviews. One such review took place in December 2012.

ai_rows.comments: Sponsor A's unsolicited phone call expressing interest in a potential acquisition; no price or specific terms.
ai_rows.source_quote: In February 2013, Stephen I. Sadove, Saks’ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer, received an unsolicited phone call from a representative of a private equity firm, which we refer to as Sponsor A, expressing interest in a potential acquisition of Saks.

flags.reason: phrase: 'February 2013'

ai_rows.comments: Hudson's Bay CEO Richard Baker met with Saks' CEO to discuss a potential acquisition; no price or specific terms.
ai_rows.source_quote: On April 1, 2013, Mr. Sadove met with Richard Baker, the Director, Governor, and Chief Executive Officer of Hudson’s Bay, at the request of Mr. Baker and discussed a potential acquisition of Saks by Hudson’s Bay. No specific proposals were made by Hudson’s Bay, and no specific transaction terms were discussed.

flags.reason: §P-R5/§E4: resolved_name="Hudson's Bay Company" for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=["Hudson's Bay"]

ai_rows.comments: Sponsor E first appears as a potential joint-acquisition partner with Sponsor A; no independent price indication for Sponsor E on this date (the $15 indication is attributed to Hudson's Bay and Sponsor A).
ai_rows.source_quote: A private equity firm that we refer to as Sponsor E and that Sponsor A had stated was in discussions with Sponsor A regarding participation in a potential joint acquisition of Saks also participated in the meeting with Sponsor A.

flags.reason: phrase: 'During the week of April 15, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-04-17

ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 process-position fallback (pre-NDA, pre-process-letter concrete indication; §C4) and §G1 informal-trigger language ('considering making an offer ... for at least $15 per share').
ai_rows.additional_note: single lower bound: at least $15/share, cash
ai_rows.comments: Pre-NDA concrete price indication (§C4). Sponsor A's NDA is executed on 4/26/2013 (later in same phase), so §P-D6 is satisfied naturally by the later NDA row; no exemption needed.
ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of April 15, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs met separately with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A in order to discuss their respective consideration of a potential acquisition of Saks. Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

flags.reason: Sponsor A communicated 'at least $15 per share' concrete price indication during week of April 15, 2013; Sponsor A's CA executed on April 26, 2013.
Single lower-bound (at least $15) per §H1.
phrase: 'During the week of April 15, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-04-17

ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 process-position fallback (pre-NDA, pre-process-letter concrete indication; §C4) and §G1 informal-trigger language ('considering making an offer ... for at least $15 per share').
ai_rows.additional_note: single lower bound: at least $15/share, cash
ai_rows.comments: Pre-NDA concrete price indication (§C4). Hudson's Bay CA executed on April 30, 2013 (later in same phase).
ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of April 15, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs met separately with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A in order to discuss their respective consideration of a potential acquisition of Saks. Each of Hudson’s Bay and Sponsor A indicated that they were considering making an offer to acquire Saks for at least $15 per share, in cash.

flags.reason: Hudson's Bay communicated 'at least $15 per share' concrete price indication during week of April 15, 2013; CA executed April 30, 2013.
Single lower-bound (at least $15) per §H1.
phrase: 'During the week of April 15, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-04-17
§P-R5/§E4: resolved_name="Hudson's Bay Company" for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=["Hudson's Bay"]

ai_rows.comments: Indicative price range communicated through Goldman Sachs.
ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of June 3, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

flags.reason: True range $15.00-$15.25/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.
phrase: 'During the week of June 3, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-06-05
§P-R5/§E4: resolved_name="Hudson's Bay Company" for 'bidder_03' not in aliases_observed=["Hudson's Bay"]

ai_rows.comments: Joint Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicative range; atomized per §E2 (filing labels them 'Sponsor A and Sponsor E' on a joint basis — emit one row per narrated constituent).
ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of June 3, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

flags.reason: True range $15.00-$16.00/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.
phrase: 'During the week of June 3, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-06-05

ai_rows.comments: Joint Sponsor A and Sponsor E indicative range; per-constituent row.
ai_rows.source_quote: During the week of June 3, 2013, representatives of Goldman Sachs spoke with representatives of each of Hudson’s Bay, Sponsor A and Sponsor E and were informed that Hudson’s Bay, on the one hand, and Sponsor A and Sponsor E, on the other hand, were each preliminarily prepared to proceed with the submission of a proposal to acquire Saks at indicative price ranges of $15 to $15.25 per share of common stock (in the case of Hudson’s Bay) and $15 to $16 per share of common stock (in the case of Sponsor A and Sponsor E).

flags.reason: True range $15.00-$16.00/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.
phrase: 'During the week of June 3, 2013'; midpoint: 2013-06-05

ai_rows.process_conditions_note: Several more weeks of due diligence required; no financing documentation; no revised merger agreement.
ai_rows.comments: Joint Sponsor E + Sponsor G final-round bid: $14.50-$15.50 range (informal per §G1 structural range signal), no merger-agreement markup, no financing support — per-constituent row per §E2.
ai_rows.source_quote: The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock, but noted that the parties would require several weeks to continue their due diligence evaluation in order to submit a more definitive proposal and did not include a revised draft merger agreement or any documents supporting availability of financing for the proposal.

flags.reason: True range $14.50-$15.50/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.

ai_rows.process_conditions_note: Several more weeks of due diligence required; no financing documentation; no revised merger agreement.
ai_rows.comments: Joint Sponsor E + Sponsor G final-round bid; per-constituent row per §E2.
ai_rows.source_quote: The joint proposal from Sponsor E and Sponsor G included an indicative price range of $14.50–$15.50 per share of common stock, but noted that the parties would require several weeks to continue their due diligence evaluation in order to submit a more definitive proposal and did not include a revised draft merger agreement or any documents supporting availability of financing for the proposal.

flags.reason: True range $14.50-$15.50/share; informal per §G1 structural signal.

ai_rows.bid_type_inference_note: Informal per §G1 process-position fallback: unsolicited first-contact letter, no NDA, no financing documentation, no merger-agreement markup, 'with no details or further information' — not a binding proposal. §D1.a first-contact exempts from §P-D6.
ai_rows.additional_note: unsolicited letter; aggregate $2.6B, no per-share, no details
ai_rows.comments: Unsolicited first-contact letter from Company H with an aggregate $2.6 billion cash offer; Goldman Sachs attempted contact multiple times without success and no further communication from Company H. Classified informal per §G1 process-position fallback (pre-process-letter for this bidder; no NDA; no financing documentation; not a binding proposal).
ai_rows.source_quote: On July 21, 2013, Saks received a letter from Company H, a privately held company based in the U.S. unknown to Saks and its advisors, purporting to propose to acquire Saks for an aggregate price of $2.6 billion in cash, with no details or further information. Goldman Sachs subsequently attempted on more than one occasion to contact the appropriate person at Company H both by telephone and by e-mail to discuss the purported offer further but was unsuccessful in making contact with such person. Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H.

flags.reason: §D1.a: unsolicited first-contact Bid, no NDA in phase, Goldman attempted contact 'but was unsuccessful' and 'Neither Saks nor Goldman Sachs received any subsequent communications from Company H'.

alex_rows.comments: In late May 2013, media reports began to appear stating that Saks had engaged Goldman Sachs to explore a potential sale of Saks.
Several weeks of DD required, no availability of financing

ai_rows.comments: Sponsor A voluntarily stepped down from primary participation in favor of Sponsor E's new partnership with Sponsor G. Sponsor A later rejoins Sponsor E around July 11 (§I2 re-engagement).
ai_rows.source_quote: In early July 2013, Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be a primary participant in a potential transaction and that Sponsor E had entered into discussions with Sponsor G, a private equity firm, regarding a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

flags.reason: phrase: 'In early July 2013'

ai_rows.comments: Sponsor G voluntarily withdrew shortly after the July 11 joint bid; Sponsor A rejoined Sponsor E as primary participant. The filing narrates this 'subsequently' after the July 11 submissions and before the next dated event (week of July 15); attributing to July 11 as the narrated day of the withdrawal.
ai_rows.source_quote: Saks was subsequently informed that Sponsor G was no longer participating in the process, and that Sponsor E would again be joined in its proposal by Sponsor A as a primary participant.

flags.reason: Filing narrates withdrawal 'subsequently' after July 11 submissions and before July 15-week events; anchored to July 11 per §B3 context anchor.

alex_rows.comments: [This clearly happened after Final Round Ann -- so the day is approximate] In early July 2013, Sponsor E informed Saks that Sponsor A was no longer intending to be a primary participant in a potential transaction and that Sponsor E had entered into discussions with Sponsor G, a private equity firm, regarding a potential joint acquisition of Saks.

ai_rows.bid_date_rough: implicit drop at process end: 2013-07-28 board approval of Hudson's Bay merger
ai_rows.comments: Implicit drop: the Sponsor A/E joint bid capped at $15.50 vs Hudson's Bay's $16; by approving the Hudson's Bay merger on July 28, the board implicitly terminated the Sponsor A/E alternative. Split per §I1 to Sponsor A and Sponsor E individually since each signed its own NDA on 4/26.
ai_rows.source_quote: In the evening of July 23, 2013, senior management of Saks met with representatives of Sponsor A and Sponsor E in connection with their evaluation of an acquisition of Saks. There was no indication from Sponsor A or Sponsor E that they were prepared to increase their indicated price beyond their initially indicated range of $14.50 to $15.50 per share of common stock, nor did it appear likely that Sponsor A and Sponsor E would be prepared to conclude their diligence more quickly than they had previously indicated.

flags.reason: Filing does not narrate an explicit drop statement; inferred from board's July 28 approval of Hudson's Bay merger and the July 23 evening meeting at which Sponsor A/E would not match.
Sponsor A/E joint proposal dropped; row split per constituent NDA (Sponsor A and Sponsor E each signed individually on 4/26).

ai_rows.bid_date_rough: implicit drop at process end: 2013-07-28 board approval of Hudson's Bay merger
ai_rows.comments: Implicit drop of Sponsor A/E joint bid; per-constituent row per §I1 consortium-drop split.
